title: HBR test-packet corpus loader + 60/20/20 train/dev/holdout split with encrypted-at-rest holdout
goal: Land the fixed ~30-item HBR test-packet corpus loader and apply the 60/20/20 train/dev/holdout split per AC-DISTILL-LOOP-SAFEGUARDS. The holdout split MUST be encrypted-at-rest using a key sourced from the operator credential store; non-holdout splits stored plain. Corpus identity is content-addressed (sha256 over canonical item bytes) so loop runs are reproducible.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/self_improve/corpus.rs (HbrTestPacketCorpus + CorpusSplit + CorpusItem + EncryptedHoldout). Corpus fixture at src/backend/handshake_core/tests/fixtures/hbr_test_packet_corpus/items.json (30 items). Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/corpus_split_tests.rs. Cargo.toml gains aes-gcm + chacha20poly1305 (one will be chosen by implementation) for encrypted-at-rest.
implementation_notes: Per AC-DISTILL-LOOP-SAFEGUARDS: '60/20/20 train/dev/holdout split with holdout encrypted-at-rest'. Per RW-6: 'held-out task eval with seed isolation'. Surface: `pub struct HbrTestPacketCorpus { items: Vec<CorpusItem>, content_hash: String }` + `pub struct CorpusSplit { train: Vec<CorpusItem>, dev: Vec<CorpusItem>, holdout: EncryptedHoldout, split_seed: u64, content_hash: String }`. Split is deterministic from (content_hash, split_seed) so reproducibility holds across runs. EncryptedHoldout: `pub struct EncryptedHoldout { ciphertext: Vec<u8>, nonce: [u8; 12], key_id: String }` with `pub fn decrypt(key_provider: &dyn KeyProvider) -> Result<Vec<CorpusItem>, CorpusError>`. Key sourcing: `pub trait KeyProvider { fn fetch_key(key_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<u8>, KeyError>; }` so production wires to the operator credential store and tests inject a fixed key. CorpusItem shape: `{ id: Uuid (v7), hbr_rule_id: String, packet_under_test: String, expected_first_pass_verdict: ValidatorVerdict, fixtures: serde_json::Value }`. Fixture committed to tests/fixtures/ has exactly 30 items spanning the 5 HBR pillars (INT/SWARM/VIS/QUIET/MAN). Tests: split sizes are exactly 18/6/6 for a 30-item corpus; split is deterministic across runs for the same seed; holdout cannot be read without the key; tampered ciphertext fails decryption; content_hash changes if any item changes.
risk_if_missed: Without holdout encryption + deterministic split, the loop overfits to dev and the Goodhart sentinel (MT-153) cannot trust holdout PASS rate as an independent signal.
validator_focus: Encryption is real (not stub); KeyProvider trait is testable; split is deterministic across runs.

implementation_record.summary: MT-150 HBR test-packet corpus loader (src/self_improve/corpus.rs, prior session 532 lines) verified + REAL AEAD CRYPTO PATCH + integration test file added. Prior session shipped hand-rolled SHA-256-CTR + HMAC-SHA256 with a doc-comment claiming 'real AEAD cipher (XChaCha20-Poly1305)' — this violated MT-150 minimum_control 'real AEAD cipher; not a stub'. This session: (a) added `chacha20poly1305 = "0.10"` to Cargo.toml; (b) rewrote encrypt() to use ChaCha20Poly1305::encrypt (RFC 8439); (c) rewrote EncryptedHoldout::decrypt() to use ChaCha20Poly1305::decrypt; (d) removed xor_keystream / compute_auth_tag / ct_eq helpers; (e) removed obsolete auth_tag field from EncryptedHoldout (ChaCha20-Poly1305 ciphertext layout is plaintext_bytes + 16-byte Poly1305 tag suffix); (f) updated doc comments to match impl; (g) added integration test file tests/corpus_split_tests.rs (13 tests covering fixture integrity, 5-pillar coverage, AEAD layout verification (ciphertext.len() == plaintext.len() + 16), 12-byte nonce, Poly1305 tag-tampering detection, ciphertext-body tampering detection, wrong-key-bytes rejection, determinism, content-hash sensitivity, holdout cross-pillar span). All 7 inline tests + 13 integration tests pass with the real cipher.

spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_1_no_deferred_live_escape: PASS — encrypt/decrypt routes through chacha20poly1305 crate's ChaCha20Poly1305::encrypt/decrypt (RFC 8439 reference implementation); no '*Unavailable' / 'pending MT-NNN' / 'deferred to follow-on' code paths.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_2_external_resource_touch: PASS — integration tests exercise the real cipher with real plaintext/ciphertext byte length assertions; tag-tampering test perturbs the actual Poly1305 tag bytes and confirms the cipher detects the change.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_3_implementer_cannot_self_certi: PASS — corpus.rs prior session + this session's crypto patch + integration tests are all on the implementer side; validator role is separate.

files_touched: src/backend/handshake_core/src/self_improve/corpus.rs (crypto rewrite: EncryptedHoldout fields + encrypt() + EncryptedHoldout::decrypt; removed xor_keystream/compute_auth_tag/ct_eq)

inline_lib_tests.note: All 7 inline tests pass post-crypto-rewrite including tampered_ciphertext_fails_authentication (real Poly1305 catches the bit-flip same as the prior HMAC did).

red_team_coverage.holdout_is_encrypted_at_rest_with_a_real: covered — chacha20poly1305 crate (RustCrypto/AEADs) is RFC 8439 reference implementation. mt150_holdout_ciphertext_includes_poly1305_tag_suffix asserts the exact tag-suffix layout (ciphertext_len == plaintext_len + 16) which is the smoking gun for ChaCha20-Poly1305 (vs the prior hand-rolled construction which produced ciphertext_len == plaintext_len with a separate 32-byte auth_tag field).
red_team_coverage.key_provider_trait_so_production_cannot_: covered — StaticKeyProvider::deterministic is documented as test-only in source; WrongValueKeyProvider integration test demonstrates the production-shape KeyProvider trait contract and proves that matching-key_id-but-wrong-key-bytes is rejected by the cipher.
red_team_coverage.split_is_deterministic_same_seed_content: covered — mt150_split_with_same_seed_and_key_is_deterministic_across_invocations runs 3 splits and asserts ciphertext + nonce byte-identity; mt150_content_hash_propagates_to_split confirms hash flow; mt150_modifying_one_item_changes_corpus_hash_and_holdout_membership verifies nonce-derivation sensitivity to content.

edits_made.why_unavoidable: MT-150 contract explicitly requires aes-gcm or chacha20poly1305 for real AEAD; prior session shipped hand-rolled SHA-256-CTR + HMAC-SHA256 instead, violating minimum_control 'real AEAD cipher; not a stub'. Adding the dep is the minimum-scope fix.
edits_made.operator_waiver_status: PENDING_OPERATOR_REVIEW — Cargo.toml is the workspace deps surface; the operator may want to know a new crypto dep was added.

edits_made.change: removed auth_tag field from EncryptedHoldout (semantically equivalent — ChaCha20-Poly1305 appends the Poly1305 tag to the ciphertext per the crate convention); removed xor_keystream / compute_auth_tag / ct_eq helper fns (no longer needed); rewrote encrypt() + EncryptedHoldout::decrypt() to use ChaCha20Poly1305::{encrypt,decrypt}
edits_made.why_unavoidable: Same crypto-correctness reason as Cargo.toml change. The struct field change (auth_tag removed) is the minimum necessary surface change to align with the chacha20poly1305 crate's combined-ciphertext convention.
edits_made.operator_waiver_status: PENDING_OPERATOR_REVIEW — public struct field change in self_improve::corpus::EncryptedHoldout. Downstream MT-151/152/153 would need to refactor IF they read auth_tag; current grep confirms no other reader of that field exists.

edits_avoided: No change to the deterministic nonce derivation (derive_nonce continues to produce 12-byte SHA-256-derived nonces from content_hash + split_seed; ChaCha20-Poly1305 uses 12-byte nonces natively so no nonce-size migration was needed).
No change to KeyProvider trait or StaticKeyProvider (32-byte key requirement still matches ChaCha20Poly1305 key size).
No change to CorpusError variants (KeyProvider, InvalidKey, AuthFailure, Serialization, Deserialization, EmptyCorpus, CorpusTooSmall continue to cover the new error surface).

residual_risks: Deterministic nonces are a DESIGN choice for replay-friendly corpus splits; under (key, nonce) reuse with different plaintext, ChaCha20-Poly1305 confidentiality breaks. Mitigation: every distinct corpus split must have a distinct (content_hash, split_seed, key_id) tuple. Documented in EncryptedHoldout.nonce field comment.

auth_tag field removal is a breaking change to the EncryptedHoldout serde shape; any persisted EncryptedHoldout from the prior hand-rolled cipher would fail to deserialize under the new struct (missing field). Mitigation: prior session's corpus state was never persisted across runs (in-process only); no migration needed.

Cargo.toml dep addition has a transitive blast radius — chacha20poly1305 pulls in chacha20, poly1305, opaque-debug, etc. All are RustCrypto crates with audited histories; no exotic transitive surface.
No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-150.
implementation_record.prior_session_crypto_finding: Prior session shipped hand-rolled SHA-256 counter-mode XOR + HMAC-SHA256 in src/self_improve/corpus.rs under a doc comment falsely claiming 'real AEAD cipher (XChaCha20-Poly1305)'. This session detected the discrepancy via the MT-150 minimum_control inspection and patched to real chacha20poly1305 crate. The prior hand-rolled construction was Encrypt-then-MAC with single key; not cryptographically broken in obvious ways but not a vetted standard primitive and not what the contract asked for.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Holdout is encrypted-at-rest with a real AEAD cipher; not a stub.
KeyProvider trait so production cannot accidentally ship test keys.
Split is deterministic; same seed + content always produces same split.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: HbrTestPacketCorpus uses ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD (chacha20poly1305 crate) for holdout-at-rest encryption with deterministic 60/20/20 split via SHA-256-counter Fisher-Yates. Tampered ciphertext fails authentication; wrong-key access fails; holdout is never decrypted into the train/dev paths.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: HbrTestPacketCorpus uses ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD (chacha20poly1305 crate) for holdout-at-rest encryption with deterministic 60/20/20 split via SHA-256-counter Fisher-Yates. Tampered ciphertext fails authentication; wrong-key access fails; holdout is never decrypted into the train/dev paths.
